Badger Grapple Red — v20.5 Battle Sprites Repaint

Repainted all 10 battle sprite files (5 archetypes x front/back) with the
same procedural shading pipeline used for the area backgrounds: multi-tone
value gradients (not flat fills), defined limbs/torso/headgear/hair,
per-archetype build variation (Top is broader-shouldered, Scramble is
leaner and more crouched, matching their in-game styles).

TECHNICAL CONSTRAINT HANDLED: this game tints wrestler sprites at runtime
per roster entry (Phaser's multiplicative setTint — e.g. Bucky Shotmaker
renders as base art x red, an evolved form renders as the SAME base art x
a different color). That means the garment region has to be painted
near-white/gray so the tint produces correct color rather than mud, and
shading has to be done via VALUE (light/dark), not hue — colored shading
would fight the tint system. Verified this holds up correctly by rendering
the same sprite under multiple different real in-game tint values before
shipping (not just checking it once in isolation).

HONEST ASSESSMENT — this is the category where the procedural approach is
weakest, and I want to be direct about that rather than oversell it.
Environment art (tiles, buildings, props) is naturally forgiving of
geometric abstraction — a shaded rectangle reads fine as "a table."
Character art has a much higher bar: a set of shaded ellipses needs to
read as a specific, recognizable athletic pose, and hand/AI-illustrated
art has an intentionality (exact proportion choices, silhouette read)
that procedural composition doesn't have. What shipped here is a real,
verified technical improvement — actual shading gradients and more
anatomical structure (headgear, hair, defined limbs) where there was
flat single-tone geometry before — but it's closer to the ceiling of
what this technique can do than the environment art was. If it doesn't
clear the bar on review, character sprites are the strongest candidate
for revisiting with a different approach later.

Verified: all 10 files confirmed 96x96 before packaging, two separate
live in-battle screenshots (different archetypes, different tint colors)
via real button-click navigation through the actual boot flow, zero
console errors both times.

Cache: ?v=205. Proof: title/note reads "v20.5 Battle Sprites Repaint".
